In the Powertrain Mix Overview, the units sold for 2020 were 15,000 electric, 5,000 hybrid, and 30,000 internal combustion,
totaling 50,000 sales with a market share of 30% and a growth rate of 10%. In 2021, units sold increased to 20,000 electric,
7,000 hybrid, and 25,000 internal combustion, leading to total sales of 52,000 with a market share of 38%. The growth rate for
this year was 12%. For 2022, the figures rose to 25,000 electric, 10,000 hybrid, and 20,000 internal combustion, totaling 55,000
sales, marking a market share of 45% and a growth rate of 18%. Total units sold comprised 10,000 electric cars at 20%, 7,000
hybrid cars at 14%, and 30,000 ICE cars at 66%, with total units sold of 52,000 at 100% and a total growth of 20% from 2020
to 2021, while from 2021 to 2022, electric growth was 25%, hybrid growth was 42%, and ICE growth was -33%. In the Dealer
Inventory Levels, for the UK in 2020, there were 12,500 units, decreasing to 11,000 in 2021 and 9,500 in 2022, with a change
of -10%, total inventory of 16,000, average price at £20K, and stock days at 45. Germany had 15,000 units in 2020, 14,000 in
2021, and 13,000 in 2022, with a -7% change, total inventory of 18,000, average price of £22K, and stock days at 50.

France recorded 11,000 units sold in 2020, increasing to 12,000 in 2021 and 11,500 in 2022, reflecting a 4% change, total
inventory of 14,000, average price of £21K, and stock days at 48. Italy's inventory decreased from 9,000 in 2020 to 8,500 in
2021 and 8,000 in 2022, with a change of -6%, total inventory of 11,000, average price of £19K, and stock days at 55. Spain's
figures were 8,000 in 2020, 7,500 in 2021, and 7,000 in 2022, showing a -7% change, total inventory of 9,500, average price of
£18K, and stock days at 60.
